title: Cissampelopsis erythrochaeta
intro: Cissampelopsis erythrochaeta, also known as Korean angelica, is a plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its unique fern-like foliage and tall, upright growth habit. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cissampelopsis erythrochaeta include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cissampelopsis erythrochaeta is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is part of the larger order Apiales, which consists of flowering plants that produce small flowers in clusters. The order Apiales is a part of the clade called Asterids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants. The clade Asterids is further classified within the larger clade called Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Cissampelopsis erythrochaeta's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
